closed_out_of_scope_records.blocker: Working OPH hadron backend on suitable hardware such as GLORB/Echosahedron.
closed_out_of_scope_records.chrome_policy: Do not use Chrome workers for backend execution.
closed_out_of_scope_records.closure: Production backend output and continuum/volume/chiral/statistical systematics are published.
closed_out_of_scope_records.closure_note: Closed as out-of-scope, not solved. The current pipeline emits no hadron predictions until a working OPH hadron backend on suitable hardware publishes production output and systematics.
closed_out_of_scope_records.falsification: Surrogate local hadron artifacts are required as if they were production QCD outputs.
closed_out_of_scope_records.title: hadron backend/systematics

closed_out_of_scope_records.chrome_policy: Do not use Chrome workers for backend execution.
closed_out_of_scope_records.closure: Compact paper states the backend output and budgets or marks hadrons out-of-scope.

closed_out_of_scope_records.falsification: Compact paper promotes hadron masses without production backend evidence.
closed_out_of_scope_records.title: nonperturbative QCD hadron branch

rows.blocker: Current source identities leave an exact free (R_{>0})^2 action on the up/down spread pair.
rows.chrome_policy: No workers needed unless a new action-breaking source observable is proposed.
rows.claim_level: closed locally as theorem-grade two-modulus non-identifiability obstruction
rows.closure: Accepted sharper-obstruction mode is met by a target-free dependency DAG, exact fiber classification, and countermodels with different mass readouts.
rows.falsification: A theorem-grade source observable built from the stated corpus selects both positive spreads while preserving the declared identities.
rows.title: Backlog theorem: source-only quark sigma/spread datum for running mass sextet

rows.blocker: No class-uniform selector or classification of every admissible public quark frame is emitted.
rows.chrome_policy: No workers needed until a new global classifier packet exists.
rows.closure: Retain the existing corpus-limited global no-go or supply a theorem covering every admissible frame class.
rows.falsification: An unclassified admissible frame invalidates any global uniqueness wording.
rows.title: Backlog theorem: global public quark-frame classifier and bridge map

rows.blocker: The generation-bundle path begins from a hand-written template and does not fix the two endpoint spans.
rows.chrome_policy: No workers needed until a source-derived generator replaces the template.
rows.claim_level: closed locally as dependent non-identifiability obstruction
rows.closure: Accepted sharper-obstruction mode is met by proving that generator splitting fixes profile rays only and leaves two positive moduli.
rows.falsification: A source-derived generator with certified sector attachment emits both absolute spans without target ancestry.
rows.title: Backlog theorem: source-only up-type quark spread and branch generator

rows.blocker: Edge statistics leave two free coefficients in sigma_u=S13+c_u delta and sigma_d=S23+c_d delta.
rows.chrome_policy: No workers needed until a new coefficient-fixing identity is proposed.
rows.claim_level: closed locally as dependent readback obstruction
rows.closure: Accepted sharper-obstruction mode is met by the target-free two-parameter countermodel family.
rows.falsification: A source identity fixes both coefficients independently without fitting quark rows.
rows.title: Backlog theorem: source-only down-type quark spread and branch generator

rows.blocker: Source physics does not choose an external running-mass chart; the light target rows use MSbar at 2 GeV.
rows.chrome_policy: No workers needed until an operational scheme selector is proposed.
rows.claim_level: closed locally as finite-renormalization non-identifiability obstruction
rows.closure: Accepted sharper-obstruction mode is met by the scheme-orbit theorem and explicit comparison-chart declaration.
rows.falsification: A source-emitted operational observable selects a unique light-quark scheme/scale representative.
rows.title: Backlog theorem: light-quark low-energy scheme and readout map

rows.blocker: Charm and bottom use self-scale MSbar coordinates while top uses a separate pole extraction; no common-scale threshold map is emitted.
rows.chrome_policy: No workers needed until a concrete RG/threshold packet exists.
rows.claim_level: closed locally as threshold/scheme non-identifiability obstruction
rows.closure: Accepted sharper-obstruction mode is met by the heavy-coordinate scheme theorem and the dimensionful mass-texture audit.
rows.falsification: A source RG trajectory, threshold map, top conversion, and same-scheme running Higgs normalization emit dimensionless physical Yukawas.
rows.title: Backlog theorem: heavy-quark matching and threshold scheme transport
